failure_surface: Output file did not match expected results despite successful code execution; test_case_results = [0]
root_cause: Creating a new workbook from scratch and copying cell-by-cell loses critical workbook properties including formulas (converted to static values), merged cells, conditional formatting, sheet-level properties, and potentially column widths/row heights. The task requires preserving these properties while deleting specific rows.
minimal_fix_hypothesis: Instead of creating a new Workbook object and copying cells, modify the existing workbook in-place by marking rows for deletion and removing them directly, then save the modified workbook. This preserves formulas, formatting, merged cells, and other sheet properties automatically.
evidence: Code explicitly creates new_wb = Workbook() and ws_new = new_wb.active instead of modifying ws_original
Cell copying uses target_cell.value = source_cell.value which extracts static values and cannot preserve formulas
Manual style copying (font, border, fill) is fragile and may miss merged cells, conditional formats, or number format variations
Test case evaluation returned 0 indicating output did not match golden reference
The instruction specified 'delete entire rows' but implementation replaced the entire sheet content structure

memory_items.title: Preserve Workbook Structure When Deleting Rows
memory_items.lesson: When deleting rows from an Excel file, modify the existing workbook in-place using delete_rows() or similar methods rather than recreating the workbook from scratch
memory_items.generalization: openpyxl worksheets support row/column deletion operations that preserve formulas, merged cells, and other workbook-level properties that are lost when copying to a new workbook

memory_items.title: Formulas Become Static Values When Recopying
memory_items.lesson: Copying cell.value from one workbook to another converts formulas to their calculated values; formulas themselves must be copied as formula strings if preservation is required
memory_items.generalization: When preserving formulas is needed, always check source_cell.is_formula or source_cell.data_type and copy the formula attribute specifically, not just the computed value

memory_items.title: Validate Output Against Expected Behavior Before Saving
memory_items.lesson: After performing bulk row deletions, verify the resulting row count and sample retained/deleted rows match expectations before writing to disk
memory_items.generalization: Implement post-processing sanity checks including row count verification, spot-checking boundary cases, and confirming deleted rows actually met all specified criteria

edits.content:
### 7. Preserving Workbook Integrity During Bulk Modifications
- **In-Place Modification:** When adding or removing rows, prefer modifying the existing worksheet in-place (using delete_rows/delete_cols or similar) rather than creating a new workbook.
- **Formula Risk:** Copying cells between workbooks converts formulas to static values. Preserve formula integrity by keeping modifications within the same workbook object.
- **Merged Cells & Formatting:** Workbook-level properties (merged cells, conditional formatting, sheet views) are often lost during recreation. Modify the original to maintain these features.
- **Validation After Deletion:** Always verify row counts and sample content after bulk deletions to ensure criteria were correctly applied.
